oecd_pi_index_change_2015_2024.method: OECD price-to-income index, % change from 2015 base

demographia_national_multiple_2024.method: median multiple (median house price / median household income)

demographia_national_multiple_2019.method: median multiple (median house price / median household income)

housing.counter_context: Prices softened modestly 2023–early 2025 as rates rose. Affordability remains near record levels; the dip does not reverse the decade trend.

tr_refusal_rate_2024.method: % of all temporary resident applications refused

immigration.counter_context: The 2024–25 refusal surge is partly deliberate policy (study-permit caps, PAL/TAL system), not pure dysfunction. This is itself a "doors closing" story — a policy reversal from the immigration-growth era.

gdp_pc_growth_2014_2023.method: real GDP per capita % growth, 2014–2023 (PPP, constant USD)

gdp_pc_pct_of_us_2014.source: Trevor Tombe / The Hub, OECD data
gdp_pc_pct_of_us_2014.method: Canada GDP/capita as % of US GDP/capita (PPP)

gdp_pc_pct_of_us_2024.source: Trevor Tombe / The Hub, OECD data
gdp_pc_pct_of_us_2024.method: Canada GDP/capita as % of US GDP/capita (PPP)

oecd_avg_crossed_year.method: year Canada GDP/capita fell below OECD average (PPP)

economy.counter_context: GDP/capita decline reflects both slow output growth and rapid population growth from immigration. Per-worker productivity numbers tell a similar story.

without_gp_2022.method: Canadians without a regular family doctor or NP

without_gp_2024.method: Canadians without a regular family doctor or NP

without_gp_pct_2022.method: % of Canadian adults without a regular primary care provider

healthcare.counter_context: The attachment gap narrowed slightly 2022→2024 despite population growth. The structural shortage persists; demand is outpacing supply.

vcsi_pct_change_2014_2024.method: % change in VCSI from 2014 low to 2024

crime.counter_context: 2024's year-over-year rise (+0.3%) was the smallest since 2014 — a possible plateau. Some violent-crime measures remain below mid-2000s peaks. The story is a sharp reversal of the decline, not an all-time high across the board.

work.note: Credential underemployment of internationally educated immigrants is well documented. Exact figures for chart use are not yet sourced to primary data. Chart will be replaced by a prose section with [Unverified] label until sourced. See spec §6.
work.counter_context: Once in Canada 10+ years, immigrant earnings converge toward Canadian-born levels. The issue is the quality and length of the gap, not permanent exclusion.

axis_notes.cost_of_living_inv: Inverted: lower cost = higher score. Source: Numbeo Cost of Living Index 2024.
axis_notes.healthcare: Source: Commonwealth Fund International Health Policy Survey 2023 / OECD Health at a Glance 2023.
axis_notes.job_market: Source: OECD Employment Outlook 2024 / World Bank.
axis_notes.quality_of_life: Source: Numbeo Quality of Life Index 2024 / Expat Insider 2024.

alternatives.normalize_note: All axes are raw scores 0–100 within a consistent source per axis. Scores normalized independently per axis (min-max across 7 countries) before plotting. Outward = better on all axes.

Portugal: EU residency via D7/NHR visa, low cost
Germany: Strong labour protections, wages matching skills
EU Blue Card pathway for skilled immigrants
UAE: Zero income tax, highest Safety Index in dataset (86)
Fastest-growing expat job market in region (World Bank 2024)
Australia: Universal Medicare, Commonwealth Fund healthcare rank 4 (2023)
Lower P/I ratio than Canada in comparable cities (Demographia 2024)
Points-based immigration with faster processing than IRCC
Japan: Tech-visa and Specified Skilled Worker pathways expanding
Mexico: Numbeo Cost of Living Index: 59% lower than Toronto
Temporary Resident card within 6 weeks (vs years in Canada)
Large remote-worker expat communities in CDMX, Oaxaca, Mérida
